source: Built from official bulk sources: goarmy cmtjobs servlet, airforce.com careers API, NAVMC 1200.1L, and DoD COOL. No figure inferred; missing fields are absent, not invented. Pay is deliberately excluded — it is a function of paygrade, not of the job.

specialties.name: Aviation Maintenance Technician
specialties.what_it_is: Aircrew duty is common — AMTs frequently fly as flight mechanics.

entry_requirements.asvab_line_score: UNVERIFIED — the Coast Guard has NO line-score composites; each rating sums raw subtest standard scores. The AMT requirement is published in Enclosure 3 of COMDTINST M1510.10, which is not publicly fetchable. AFQT minimum to enlist is 32.

entry_requirements.asvab_line_score: Coast Guard has no line-score system — ratings sum raw ASVAB subtest standard scores (per data/interest-taxonomy.json: 'USCG: no line score — per-rating subtest sums'). One secondary source (study.com, searched 2026-07-12) reports BM A-School requires AR+VE ≥ 100 with no separate minimum on any individual subtest. UNVERIFIED at primary-source level — not independently confirmed via a direct fetch of an official uscg.mil/gocoastguard.com rating-requirements page this session. The Coast Guard's general enlistment floor is an AFQT of 32 (50+ for GED holders), per a different secondary source in the same search; this is the enlistment gate, not the BM-specific rating gate. Do NOT invent a named composite — Coast Guard, like Navy, uses raw subtest sums per rating, not a composite name.
entry_requirements.security_clearance: Not typically required for the general rating; law-enforcement/homeland-security-adjacent boat-crew duties (e.g., maritime law enforcement boardings) may carry additional screening, not independently confirmed to primary-source level this session.

specialties.name: Culinary Specialist
specialties.what_it_is: NAMING NOTE: The Coast Guard renamed this rating from 'Food Service Specialist (FS)' to 'Culinary Specialist (CS)' on 2017-01-06, to better reflect the culinary skill level involved (per Wikipedia summary of official Coast Guard messaging, cross-referenced against gocoastguard.com/careers/enlisted/cs which uses 'CS' and 'Culinary Specialist' throughout). data/interest-taxonomy.json still lists this rating as 'FS — Food Service Specialist,' which is the pre-2017 name; recommend the taxonomy file be updated to 'CS — Culinary Specialist' in a future pass. Flagged here rather than silently corrected.

entry_requirements.asvab_line_score: UNVERIFIED — no rating-specific composite score located this session. Coast Guard uses summed raw subtest scores per rating (like the Navy) rather than a single published line score, consistent with data/interest-taxonomy.json's 'no line score — per-rating subtest sums' note for this cluster. The general Coast Guard AFQT floor is 32 with a high school diploma (50 with a GED), lowered from 40 in November 2023, but this is the general accession floor, not a CS-specific rating cutoff. Confirm the current CS-specific subtest requirement with a recruiter.

specialties.name: Information Systems Technician
specialties.what_it_is: U.S. citizenship required. The Coast Guard is a Department of Homeland Security component, not DoD - administrative and benefit specifics can differ from the other five branches even where dollar figures happen to align.

entry_requirements.asvab_line_score: AR + MK + EI + GS >= 171 (Arithmetic Reasoning + Mathematics Knowledge + Electronics Information + General Science subtest sum). The Coast Guard has no named 'line score' composite system, consistent with data/interest-taxonomy.json's note. Search-sourced (ratemyasvab.com, operationmilitarykids.org); not independently fetched to primary-source (uscg.mil) confidence this session.
entry_requirements.security_clearance: Not independently confirmed to a specific tier this session. The rating description ('system administrator for unclassified and classified systems') implies at least a Secret clearance is required for many IT billets - flagged as inference, not a directly sourced clearance-level statement. Whatever tier applies, clearance eligibility is a hard gate independent of ASVAB score: per SEAD 4 (the government-wide National Security Adjudicative Guidelines, applied DHS-wide including the Coast Guard), common disqualifiers include foreign influence/preference (close foreign-national contacts, dual citizenship, foreign-government ties), financial considerations (unresolved delinquent debt, bankruptcy pattern, tax delinquency), and drug involvement/substance misuse (recent illegal drug use or a pattern of misuse) - a qualifying aptitude score does not offset a disqualifying finding under these guidelines.

specialties.name: Intelligence Specialist
specialties.what_it_is: Under DoD's 13 Adjudicative Guidelines (Security Executive Agent Directive 4 / 32 CFR Part 147), none of the following are automatic mechanical disqualifiers under the 'whole person' standard, but they are the dominant real-world reasons applicants and incumbents are denied or lose eligibility, and each can rule out this specialty even with a fully qualifying ASVAB score: (1) unresolved foreign contacts, foreign relatives in certain countries, foreign property/financial interests, or foreign romantic ties that create potential foreign leverage (Guideline B, Foreign Influence); (2) a pattern of serious financial delinquency -- unresolved debt (especially over $10,000), recent bankruptcy, wage garnishment, or repeated late payment patterns (Guideline F; financial issues are cited as roughly 45% of DoD clearance denials); (3) illegal drug use, misuse of prescription medication, or continued use after applying (Guideline H); and (4), treated especially seriously, deliberately concealing or falsifying any of the above -- including an UNREPORTED dual citizenship or undisclosed foreign contact -- during the application or investigation (Guideline E, Personal Conduct). A 16-24-year-old considering this cluster should treat clearance eligibility as a real, independent gate, not a formality layered on top of a qualifying ASVAB score.

entry_requirements.asvab_line_score: UNVERIFIED at the rating-specific line-score level. The Coast Guard's general enlistment AFQT minimum is 40 -- the highest floor of any branch (per search results this session and data/branch-profiles.json) -- but a rating-specific IS line-score/composite requirement was not located this session; gocoastguard.com/careers/enlisted/is was fetched directly this session and did not surface a specific numeric requirement in the fetched content.
entry_requirements.security_clearance: UNVERIFIED at the specific-tier level (Secret vs. TS/SCI) this session. The IS rating page fetched this session describes the role as requiring personnel who are 'comfortable keeping secrets' and whose training includes 'protecting classified information,' consistent with this cluster's pattern across every other branch (TS/SCI, SSBI-adjudicated), but the fetched page did not state an explicit clearance tier -- treat TS/SCI as the working assumption pending direct recruiter confirmation.

specialties.name: Maritime Enforcement Specialist
specialties.what_it_is: Valid state driver's license required, plus a minimum 24 months of obligated service remaining from the date of ME 'A' School graduation, per forcecom.uscg.mil.

entry_requirements.asvab_line_score: No ME-specific ASVAB subtest requirement was located this session; only the general Coast Guard AFQT floor (reported as 32 with a diploma, per data/branch-profiles.json's general_eligibility.education note) applies as the general accession gate. UNVERIFIED at the rating-specific level.
entry_requirements.security_clearance: Background investigation/security clearance required; no conviction for domestic violence and no restraining order that would prohibit firearm possession — a specific ME eligibility gate, since MEs must be armed to perform the rating's core boarding-officer duties (per gocoastguard.com).

Must be a U.S. citizen and able to meet all security clearance requirements.
